rationale: Ground truth here is thin (mostly social comments plus one inline concern about reduce_scatter test coverage that the diff already resolves), so recall is low for both, but X explicitly engages with that exact assertion block while Y never mentions it. X delivers five concrete, grounded, file/line-anchored findings (untested early-return branch, dropped cfg_parallel_size guard, a plausible unconditional-collective-call issue, missing docstrings) that are all actionable. Y is well-grounded and technically sound (verifies DPMetadata, cleanup, group separation against real vLLM source) but yields only one vague 'worth a comment' suggestion, so despite slightly higher precision it trails badly on actionability and recall.
